summary: SPARK's netcode is a host-authoritative star over Trystero 0.25 (@trystero-p2p/{core,nostr,torrent,mqtt}) with simultaneous multi-strategy broadcast: nostr eager-primary (7 pinned relays), torrent dynamic-import fallback (3 WSS trackers), mqtt default-OFF. NetTransport (src/net/transport.ts) dedups peers/messages across strategies, validates every inbound payload via parseNetMessage against PROTOCOL_VERSION=7 and a tsc-mirrored GameAction allowlist, and latches+drops peers whose HELLO carries a mismatched protoVersion. The host mints a stable lobby seat-map (LOBBY_PRESENCE beacons) compacted to a dense roster at Begin Match (START_GAME_SIGNAL), freezes peerId→seat for anti-spoof INTENT stamping, and emits NetSnapshot every 6 physics ticks (60 Hz sim / 10 Hz snapshots); clients lerp-interpolate over 100 ms and send seq-numbered INTENTs. The S79 P4 TOFU latch (session.hostPeerId, set from the first roster-bearing message whose seat-0 entry names the sender, or first NETSNAPSHOT) gates the five host-authored kinds on the client and doubles as 3+player host-loss detection; its documented ceiling is that a spoofer beating the genuine host's first message wins the latch — crypto identity is explicitly deferred to backlog #4. There is no host-migration, no reconnect, and no mid-game rejoin: connection loss shows a full-screen overlay whose only exit is teardownNet + RETURN_TO_TITLE; a mid-game client drop on the host side leaves the player's avatar/structures in the sim untouched (no bench/removal logic exists). Trystero's selfId is a Math.random 20-char per-page-load constant — not cryptographic — and signaling is AES-GCM encrypted with a key derived from empty-password+appId+roomCode, so room secrecy is only the 6-char code; DTLS encrypts the data channels but provides zero application-level identity.
key_facts: Transport strategy: nostr is eager/always-on primary (src/net/transport.ts:292-301), torrent + mqtt dynamic-imported behind STRATEGY_FLAGS {nostr:true, torrent:true, mqtt:false} (src/net/iceConfig.ts:69-73); all enabled strategies broadcast SIMULTANEOUSLY and the app layer dedups (transport.ts:10-16).

joinRoom config = { appId:'spark-game-v1' (iceConfig.ts:30), relayConfig:{urls, redundancy:urls.length}, rtcConfig:{iceServers (2 Google STUN + 3 openrelay.metered.ca TURN, iceConfig.ts:77-95), iceTransportPolicy:'all'}, trickleIce:true } — NO password field (transport.ts:367-405). Trystero derives the AES-GCM signaling key from `password ?? ''`+appId+roomId (node_modules/@trystero-p2p/core/dist/strategy.mjs:84, crypto.mjs genKey via SHA-256), so anyone with the bundle's appId + a room code can read/forge signaling.

NOSTR_RELAYS = 7 pinned relays (nos.lol, relay.mostr.pub, purplerelay.com, nostr.mom, offchain.pub, nostr-pub.wellorder.net, relay.primal.net) rotated S44 (iceConfig.ts:37-45); TORRENT_TRACKERS = 3 WSS trackers (iceConfig.ts:55-59); HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS=30000, ICE_POLL_* 1s/30s (iceConfig.ts:97-99).

Peer id source: Trystero selfId re-exported at transport.ts:64; it is `genId(20)` using Math.random over a 62-charset, a module-level const = stable per page load, identical across strategies (node_modules/@trystero-p2p/core/dist/utils.mjs:6-7). NOT cryptographic, NOT persistent across refresh (Trystero assigns fresh peerId on refresh — transport.ts:443-449), and unauthenticated: WebRTC DTLS encrypts channels but gives no identity guarantee.

NetTransport API: connect(roomCode), send(msg) broadcast on every active strategy, on(handler), onPeerChange(handler) deduped once per peerId across strategies, peerCount(), peerIds() in Set-insertion (join) order (transport.ts:590-592), isConnected(), getDiagnostics(), disconnect(), onError, onProtocolMismatch (transport.ts:27-37,153-194).

Receive path handleRawMessage (transport.ts:230-277): JSON.parse → protocolMismatchPeers drop-latch → detectProtocolMismatch sniff BEFORE parseNetMessage → parseNetMessage null-reject → fan-out. Mismatched-HELLO peers are latched and ALL their later messages dropped (transport.ts:176-194); latch cleared on full peer-leave (transport.ts:449) and disconnect (transport.ts:658).

PROTOCOL_VERSION = 7 as const (src/net/protocol.ts:59); bump history v1→7 documented in comments at protocol.ts:26-58 (S52 v3 PLACE_FROM_FREE, S62 v4 roster, S71 v5 TRIGGER_BOMB, S75 v6 TRIGGER_RAINBOW, S77 v7 seagull). Bump procedure: no back-compat shim, stale peers hard-rejected at HELLO, both sides deploy together; HelloMsg.protoVersion is the literal type 7 so raising PROTOCOL_VERSION without updating it errors at buildHello's return (protocol.ts:120-127 tsc tripwire). Host-internal actions get no bump; new client intents / desync-visible changes do (precedent ladder in protocol.ts:44-58).

Message-kind union NetMessage = HELLO | INTENT | NETSNAPSHOT | START_GAME_SIGNAL | ENDGAME | GODLY_TRIGGER | LOBBY_PRESENCE (protocol.ts:215-222). parseNetMessage validates: HELLO protoVersion strict-equal, INTENT.action.type in KNOWN_GAME_ACTION_TYPES_RECORD (compile-time mirror of GameAction['type'], protocol.ts:266-338), NETSNAPSHOT.snapshot.schemaVersion === 1 (WIRE_SCHEMA_VERSION, protocol.ts:343,420-421), roster validated fail-closed ≤ MAX_PLAYERS (isValidRoster, protocol.ts:359-375).

Room codes: 6 chars from 32-char alphabet '23456789ABCDEFGHJKLMNPQRSTUVWXYZ' generated by Math.random — explicitly 'fine for friends-only matchmaking' (protocol.ts:229-237); parseRoomCode canonicalizes (protocol.ts:243-250). Room secrecy is the ONLY access control.

Host flow (src/net/hostHandlers.ts:135-206): createHostStartHandler makes NetTransport+HostSync, wireHelloOnJoin sends HELLO on every peer join (hostHandlers.ts:104-112), onPeerChange reconciles session.lobbySeats + broadcasts LOBBY_PRESENCE + local self-dispatch (hostHandlers.ts:179-184); transport.on applies only INTENT, stamping action.playerId from session.hostSeats.get(peerId) via stampSenderSeat (hostHandlers.ts:191-200, src/net/intentStamp.ts:23-25) — a client can only act as its own seat; unknown peer applies as-is (legacy 2-player path).

Begin Match (hostHandlers.ts:213-259): reconcile then DENSE-compact via buildMatchRoster (contiguous seats 0..N-1 required by radialSpawnPos), freeze hostSeats BEFORE sending START_GAME_SIGNAL {mode:'1v1', roster} then local START_GAME dispatch. Excess peers beyond MAX_PLAYERS=6 (src/constants.ts:61) are left unseated and dropped with a console.warn (hostHandlers.ts:240-244).

Seat stability: session.lobbySeats is the single source of truth (src/net/session.ts:45, src/net/lobbyRoster.ts:58-87) — survivors keep seats, departures leave holes, new peers take lowest free seat; an unfilled hole at Begin compacts and shifts higher seats down (documented one-time colour shift; the shift-free sparse-seat variant is explicitly 'deferred to the netcode-infra backlog', lobbyRoster.ts:25-29).

Snapshot cadence: SNAPSHOT_INTERVAL_TICKS = round(PHYSICS_HZ/NET_SNAPSHOT_HZ) = 60/10 = 6 (src/main.ts:125); host sends in PLAYING|WIN|POSTGAME when tick-lastSnapshotTick >= 6 (main.ts:820-832). HostSync.buildSnapshotMessage increments snapshotSeq monotonically (src/net/sync.ts:25-41); ClientSync.receive rejects snapshotSeq <= lastSeq (sync.ts:61-69); intents use an independent intentSeq counter (sync.ts:75-78); interpolation over NET_INTERPOLATION_MS=100 (constants.ts:386, sync.ts:98-188).

Client dispatcher (main.ts:264-284): joiner wraps actions as INTENT via clientSync.wrapIntent + transport.send, with optimistic local apply for PREDICTABLE_ACTIONS; client never steps physics (!isClient gates at main.ts:551-552 etc.).

S79 P4 TOFU latch hostAuthFilter (src/net/clientHandlers.ts:61-83): latches session.hostPeerId from the first LOBBY_PRESENCE/START_GAME_SIGNAL whose seat-0 roster entry NAMES THE SENDER, else first NETSNAPSHOT; then NETSNAPSHOT/GODLY_TRIGGER/START_GAME_SIGNAL/ENDGAME/LOBBY_PRESENCE from any other peer are dropped (fail-closed pre-latch). Stops: spoofed high-snapshotSeq wedge, fake ENDGAME win, START_GAME_SIGNAL seat hijack, bogus GODLY_TRIGGER (clientHandlers.ts:41-59). Documented ceiling: a spoofer who BEATS the genuine host's first message into the victim's handler wins the latch — requires joining the secret-coded room and racing the host's join-triggered beacon; accepted until crypto identity lands (clientHandlers.ts:54-59, session.ts:47-57, HANDOFF_2026-06-10_S79-S80.md:35).

Host-loss today (main.ts:966-996): connectionLost = isNetworked && PLAYING && (peerCount()===0 || hostLost), where hostLost = !isHost && hostPeerId latched && hostPeerId not in peerIds() (closes the S62 3+player client limbo). Edge-triggered cinematic abort + GODLY_ABORT on transition. Overlay (src/render/connectionLostOverlay.ts:22-76) = full-screen modal, single 'Return to Title' button → teardownNet + dispatch RETURN_TO_TITLE (main.ts:419-422); teardownNet disconnects transport, resets clientSync, clears hostSeats/lobbySeats/hostPeerId (session.ts:87-113). LOCKED §13.7: 'No host-migration … both players must return to title + reconnect with new room code' and 'No reconnect. Session ends on disconnect.' (LOCKED_DECISIONS.md:586-595).

Mid-game peer drop (non-host): NO handling exists — main.ts registers no onPeerChange; hostSeats stays frozen; the dropped player's avatar/structures persist in the host sim as a ghost (no bench/removal action anywhere in src/net or main.ts). Host overlay fires only at peerCount()===0.

HELLO is transport-layer only: buildHello carries playerId/color but 'no receiver reads them — host/client message handlers ignore kind HELLO; carried for a future identity/colour handshake' (protocol.ts:80-84). E2E override seam window.__TEST_PROTO_VERSION_OVERRIDE__ lets a test peer announce a stale version (protocol.ts:104-127).

e2e net infra: e2e/nplayer.spec.ts has (a) 4-peer real-Trystero/Nostr FFA test tagged @quarantine-flaky (nplayer.spec.ts:58-156), (b) deterministic single-page 6-player render+pixel proof (gating, nplayer.spec.ts:158-280), (c) S70 2-peer real-WebRTC lobby-presence test also @quarantine-flaky (nplayer.spec.ts:282-334). e2e.yml splits a GATING lane (--grep-invert @quarantine-flaky) from a NON-GATING continue-on-error quarantine lane with separate --output dir (.github/workflows/e2e.yml:79-98). Real 6-peer WebRTC explicitly rejected by Council (15 data channels under swiftshader = flake; nplayer.spec.ts:22-25). playwright.config.ts: workers:1, port 5173, swiftshader, mocked transport rejected (C9, playwright.config.ts:16-18).

LOCKED_DECISIONS net sections: §13.1 transport + relay-pin NOTE v4/v5 with future-bump relay re-audit (LOCKED_DECISIONS.md:25-114,513-521), §13.2 authority model (524-533), §13.3 sync protocol per-direction seqs (535-550), §13.4 room codes (552-559), §13.7 known v1 limits incl. no host-migration/no reconnect/tab-hidden host pause (582-595), §13.18 ENDGAME both-halves wire rule: every new NetMessage kind MUST verify send call site AND recv dispatch before merge (1013-1023). Host-migration prior art = deliberately deferred stub (Grok R2 advocated, Gemini overlay-v1 adopted; LOCKED_DECISIONS.md:510-511, BACKLOG.md:321,442-446); NO stub code exists.

WebCrypto: zero crypto.subtle usage in src/ (grep: only the TOFU ceiling comment mentions crypto). Trystero core already requires WebCrypto (crypto.subtle SHA-256/AES-GCM + crypto.getRandomValues, @trystero-p2p/core/dist/crypto.mjs) — available only in secure contexts. Both shipping surfaces are secure contexts: https://spark-online.space (LOCKED_DECISIONS.md:604-606) and http://localhost:5173 dev/e2e (playwright.config.ts:37,64-69). Plain-http LAN-IP access (vite --host) would lack crypto.subtle.

Bundle charter: 550 KB soft cap (raised from 500 in S57, HANDOFF_2026-05-30_S57.md:15); current main bundle 534.5 KB → ~15.5 KB headroom (.claude/session-state.json:282, HANDOFF_2026-06-10_S81.md:13).

Backlog #4 ground truth (boot-snapshot.md:13,20): 'netcode infra — host-migration / reconnect / 6-player hardening / crypto peer identity to lift the S79 P4 TOFU sender-auth ceiling'. No design or stub exists in code; clientHandlers.ts:57-59 and lobbyRoster.ts:29 both point at this backlog item.

Diagnostics surfaces: NetDiagnostics {accepted, rejected, lastSeq, lastKind, strategies[] with per-relay connected} (transport.ts:83-89,598-639); lobby strips render sync counts + strategy summary for joiner and host (main.ts:1014-1057); world.diagnostics.raceRejects counts host-side intent races (hostHandlers.ts:186-190).

key_files.role: Multi-strategy Trystero adapter: NetTransport class, peer/message dedup, protocol-mismatch latch, diagnostics, selfId re-export
Wire protocol: PROTOCOL_VERSION=7, NetMessage union, buildHello, parseNetMessage validator, room-code gen/parse, action allowlist
APP_ID, NOSTR_RELAYS (7), TORRENT_TRACKERS (3), STRATEGY_FLAGS, ICE_SERVERS (STUN+TURN), timeouts, classifyJoinError
HostSync (snapshotSeq emit) + ClientSync (seq-gated receive, intentSeq, lerp interpolation, drag-lock preservation)
Host lobby/match factories: room create, HELLO wiring, LOBBY_PRESENCE broadcast, INTENT seat-stamping, Begin Match roster mint
Joiner factory + S79 P4 hostAuthFilter TOFU latch gating the 5 host-authored kinds; seat adoption from roster
Pure seat authority: reconcileLobbySeats (stable, holes), buildLobbyRoster (stable projection), buildMatchRoster (dense compaction)
stampSenderSeat — host-side anti-spoof playerId override on inbound INTENTs
Orchestration: client INTENT dispatcher (264-284), snapshot emit loop (820-832), client interpolation gate (928-934), connection-lost/hostLost detection (966-996), teardown paths (411-422,453-461)
Full-screen CONNECTION LOST modal; only exit is Return-to-Title
Multi-peer e2e: 4-peer real-WebRTC FFA (@quarantine-flaky), deterministic 6-player render proof (gating), S70 2-peer presence test (@quarantine-flaky)
e2e harness: hostNewRoom, joinRoom, readWorldState/readSeats/waitForWorld/waitForSeats via __SPARK__ DEV global
CI: gating lane vs non-gating @quarantine-flaky lane (separate output dir, continue-on-error)
§13.1-13.9 net locks: relays, authority model, sync seqs, room codes, no-migration/no-reconnect v1 limits, §13.18 both-halves wire rule
Unit tests for hostAuthFilter latch order/fail-closed/drop semantics

constraints: NetSnapshot wire bytes are LOCKED: snapshot.schemaVersion must stay 1 (protocol.ts:343,420-421) and netSnapshot()/applyNetSnapshot in src/state/save.ts define the byte shape — any new field is a wire change requiring deliberate review; fouledPrimitives precedent shows probing the serializer FIRST before assuming a bump is needed (HANDOFF S79-S80 P2).

Any wire-incompatible change (new client intent, desync-visible state) requires bumping PROTOCOL_VERSION (7→8) AND HelloMsg.protoVersion literal in lockstep (tsc tripwire at protocol.ts:120-127); no back-compat shims — stale peers are hard-rejected at HELLO; single-deploy lockstep is the locked procedure (protocol.ts:42-58).

RNG streams are locked: the sim is deterministic, host-authoritative, fixed-tick (PHYSICS_HZ=60, constants.ts:151); S81 P3 lesson 'hash already-serialized state before adding seeded streams' — any identity/handshake work must NOT consume from or reorder sim RNG streams or alter tick scheduling.

START_GAME_SIGNAL roster must remain an ORDERED array (never a Map) and seats contiguous 0..N-1 at Begin — radialSpawnPos assumes contiguity; byte-identical initial worlds across clients depend on it (protocol.ts:155-166, lobbyRoster.ts:108-119).
KNOWN_GAME_ACTION_TYPES_RECORD must exhaustively mirror GameAction['type'] in both directions (tsc-enforced, protocol.ts:253-265).
New NetMessage kinds must ship BOTH halves — send call site and recv dispatch — verified before merge (LOCKED §13.18, LOCKED_DECISIONS.md:1023).

test contracts: clientHandlers.test.ts locks hostAuthFilter latch/drop semantics; transport.test.ts/protocol.test.ts lock detectProtocolMismatch + parseNetMessage; nplayerSeating.test.ts locks N=2..6 seating determinism; e2e gating lane must stay green — new e2e tests gate by default unless explicitly tagged @quarantine-flaky (e2e.yml:18-31).
Mocked/deterministic transport in e2e is REJECTED (playwright.config C9, playwright.config.ts:16-18; e2e.yml:28-30) — real Trystero/Nostr P2P is the surface under test.
Bundle charter: main bundle must stay <550 KB; current 534.5 KB leaves ~15.5 KB — a crypto-identity dependency (e.g. a signature lib) must fit this or use built-in WebCrypto (zero bytes).
1v1 NOTE: transport.send() broadcasts to ALL peers; current code relies on broadcast==targeted semantics in places (hostHandlers.ts:97-103) — per-peer targeted send does not exist yet.
PRIME-AUDIT Δ1 invariant: handlers must read session.* at invocation time, never capture transport/sync refs at factory time (session.ts:11-16) — any reconnect work must preserve this.
teardownNet must clear hostPeerId (stale latch would drop ALL of a new host's messages on rejoin — session.ts:109-111); any reconnect path must re-latch fresh.
risks: TOFU race ceiling is real and accepted: a hostile peer in the room who beats the host's first roster-bearing message (or first NETSNAPSHOT, the weaker fallback that does NOT check roster seat-0 consistency — clientHandlers.ts:72-74) permanently wins the victim's latch for that session.

selfId is Math.random-generated in Trystero core (utils.mjs:6-7) and unauthenticated — a modified client can claim any peerId at the signaling layer; the host's hostSeats anti-spoof stamping keys off this same unauthenticated peerId, so seat identity ultimately rests on signaling-layer honesty plus room-code secrecy.

Signaling encryption key derives from empty password + public appId + 6-char room code (strategy.mjs:84) — the room code is the entire access-control and confidentiality boundary; ~32^6 ≈ 1.07e9 space generated by Math.random (protocol.ts:230-236).
selfId is per-page-load: a refresh after disconnect gets a NEW peerId, so any reconnect design cannot rely on Trystero ids for seat re-binding — this is the core gap a crypto identity must fill.
Mid-game non-host peer drop leaves a ghost player in the host sim (no removal/bench code path) — 6-player hardening must decide this behavior; nothing exists today.

Public relay decay is a recurring operational risk (S44 rotated 4 dead relays out; RELAY_HEALTH.md runbook referenced at iceConfig.ts:35) — host-migration/reconnect designs that re-signal through relays inherit it.

Real multi-peer e2e is quarantined non-gating due to CI flake (4-peer + S70 presence tests) — runtime verification of any host-migration work will face the same swiftshader/CI-network flake class; the gating proof layer is deterministic single-page only.
Messages sent before any strategy is ready are silently dropped (transport.ts:566-570) — reconnect handshakes must tolerate this startup window.
Tab-hidden host pause (LOCKED §13.7) freezes the sim and stalls all clients — indistinguishable from host loss to a client without further signals.

hostLost detection requires the latch: a client that never received any host-authored message before the host died (latch still null) falls back to the peerCount()===0 gate only — in a 3+player room with surviving clients it would NOT show the overlay (main.ts:975-982).
open_questions: Does applyNetSnapshot on a late/mid-game joiner (who never got START_GAME_SIGNAL) actually flip its world to PLAYING, or does it strand in LOBBY? The code paths suggest stranding (START_GAME_SIGNAL gated on gameState==='LOBBY', clientHandlers.ts:144) but no test or doc pins the late-join behavior — relevant to any rejoin-same-room-code design.

Trystero room semantics on re-join with the same room code from the same page (selfId unchanged, module const): hostHandlers comments call same-peerId rejoin 'rare Trystero behavior' (transport.ts:191-193) — empirical behavior of leave()+joinRoom with identical selfId is unverified.

Whether @trystero-p2p 0.25's createPasswordHandshake (strategy.mjs:265) performs any peer-identity binding beyond payload encryption with the empty-password key — the handshake module wasn't traced end-to-end.
Exact current gzip bundle figure for the net chunk specifically (only aggregate 534.5 KB main bundle is recorded); torrent strategy is a separate dynamic chunk, size not measured here.
MQTT strategy is compiled-in but default-OFF and has an empty relay list at startStrategy (transport.ts:333) — whether it works at all if flipped on is untested ground truth.
